name: FR Amazone
events: 11 May 1940 Amazone (Lt. R.H.G. Richard) fires two torpedoes at a submarine off the Dutch coast in position 52°55'N, 04°28'E. Both German U-boat U-7 and HMS Shark reported being missed by torpedoes.
26 Feb 1943 Amazone arrived at Bermuda from Dakar.
28 Feb 1943 Amazone departed Bermuda for the Philadelphia Navy Yard.
14 Oct 1943 Amazone departed the Philadelphia Navy Yard, USA for New London, Connecticut, USA.
16 Oct 1943 Amazone arrived at New London, Connecticut, USA from the Philadelphia Navy Yard, USA.

12 Feb 1944 Amazone departed Bermuda for Key West.
7 Oct 1944 Amazone departed the Philadelphia Navy Yard, USA for New London, Connecticut, USA.
8 Oct 1944 Amazone arrived at New London, Connecticut, USA from the Philadelphia Navy Yard, USA.
15 Oct 1944 Amazone grounded inside Fort Pond Bay, Block Island sound.
16 Oct 1944 Amazone was refloated and towed from Fort Pond Bay, Block Island sound to New London, Connecticut.
21 Oct 1944 Amazone departed New London, Connecticut, USA for the Philadelphia Navy Yard for repairs. She was towed by USS ATR-8.
23 Oct 1944 Amazone arrived at the Philadelphia Navy Yard from New London, Connecticut for repairs.
7 Dec 1944 Amazone departed the Philadelphia Navy Yard, USA for Key West, Florida, USA.
12 Dec 1944 Amazone arrived at Key West, Florida, USA from the Philadelphia Navy Yard.
12 Jul 1945 Amazone departed Key West, Florida, USA for Oran, Algeria via Bermuda and Ponta Delgada, Azores.
23 Jul 1945 Amazone departed Bermuda for Oran, Algeria via Ponta Delgada, Azores.
7 Aug 1945 Amazone departed Bermuda for Casablanca, French Morocco.
19 Aug 1945 Amazone arrived at Casablanca, French Morocco from Bermuda.
